title: Simit Sesame Ring Bread

seo.title: Turkish Simit Recipe: Authentic Sesame Ring Bread
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Turkish Simit with this easy recipe! Enjoy the classic sesame ring bread, perfect for breakfast or a snack. Get the best Turkish Simit recipe today!
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Simit Recipe: Sesame Ring Bread
h2Headings: What is Simit Sesame Ring Bread?
Pro Tips for the Best Simit Results
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Turkish Simit sesame ring bread, golden brown and covered in sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the irresistible aroma and taste of homemade Turkish Simit, a beloved sesame ring bread perfect for any time of day!

description: Simit is a quintessential Turkish sesame bread ring, known for its distinctive molasses glaze and generous coating of toasted sesame seeds. This beloved street food offers a delightful contrast of a slightly crisp exterior and a soft, chewy interior, making it an irresistible treat. Perfect for breakfast or a snack, Simit brings the authentic taste of Turkey right to your kitchen.

instructions.title: Activate Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare Dough
instructions.text: Add salt and olive oil to the yeast mixture. Gradually add the flour, mixing with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape Simit
instructions.text: Gently deflate the risen dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a long rope, about 18-20 inches (45-50 cm). Fold each rope in half and twist the two strands together, then join the ends to form a ring.

instructions.title: Prepare Dip and Coat
instructions.text: In a shallow dish, whisk together grape molasses and water. In another shallow dish, spread the toasted sesame seeds. Dip each shaped simit into the molasses mixture, ensuring it's fully coated, then immediately transfer to the sesame seeds, pressing gently to adhere the seeds evenly.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Bake
instructions.text: Place the coated simit on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Let them rest for 15-20 minutes. Preheat your oven to 400F (200C). Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown and crisp.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the baked simit to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm or at room temperature.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, use real Turkish grape molasses (pekmez). If unavailable, date molasses or even a mix of honey and water can be used as a substitute, though the taste will vary slightly.
Ensure your sesame seeds are well-toasted for a deeper, nuttier flavor. You can lightly toast them in a dry pan over medium heat until fragrant before coating.
Simit is best enjoyed fresh on the day it's baked. If you have leftovers, you can refresh them by warming them slightly in a toaster or oven.
article: Simit Sesame Ring Bread is a beloved Turkish Baking dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
